name: Etang d'Ayes – hike starting from Lac Bethmale – Réserve domaniale du mont Valier – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
name_original: Etang d'Ayes – randonnée au départ du Lac Bethmale – Réserve domaniale du mont Valier – Parc naturel régional des Pyrénées Ariégeoises
region_intro: Hiking around Bethmale offers diverse terrain within the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park. The region features the emerald waters of Lac de Bethmale, surrounded by ancient beech forests, and dramatic rocky landscapes. Mountain passes like Col de la Core provide extensive views of the Pyrenees, while valleys and cirques host waterfalls and additional ponds. This area is characterized by its mountainous environment, lush forests, and varied elevation. Best hiking trails around Bethmale The most popular hiking route is Etang d'Ayes – hike starting from Lac Bethmale, a 7.7 miles (12.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 57 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain and leads to the picturesque Étang d'Ayes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Lake Eychelle and Eychelle Hut Trail, a moderate 5.3 miles (8.5 km) path. This trail offers views of Lake Eychelle and its surrounding mountainous landscape, often taking around 4 hours 20 minutes. Local hikers also love the Ayes Pond – Lake Bethmale loop from Étang de Bethmale, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain including forests and mountain paths, often completed in about 5 hours 34 minutes. Hiking around Bethmale is defined by glacial lakes, Pyrenees mountains, beech forests, and mountain passes. The network of trails offers options for various ability levels, from easy strolls around the lake to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Bethmale are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Bethmale's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Bethmale?
A: Bethmale offers a diverse network of over 35 hiking trails. These include 4 easy routes, 16 moderate trails, and 16 more challenging options, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Bethmale?
A: Yes, Bethmale has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. The most accessible is the flat, shaded path that encircles &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, taking about 45 minutes to complete. This route offers picturesque views and picnic spots. Additionally, there are 4 easy routes available in the region.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while hiking in Bethmale?
A: Bethmale is rich in natural beauty. You can explore the emerald waters of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, discover the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/673275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayes Pond&lt;/a&gt;, or enjoy panoramic views from mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/211841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Core Pass&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features the Cirque de Campuls with its waterfall, and the stunning &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6074098" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Lake Areau&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes around Lake Bethmale?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes around Lake Bethmale. A popular moderate option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10530688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale loop from Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 12.9 km. For a slightly shorter moderate loop, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39236645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale – Étang de Bethmale loop from Étang de Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, spanning about 9.5 km.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Bethmale?
A: The best time to hike in Bethmale is typically from spring to autumn. Spring brings vibrant greens, while autumn transforms the beech forests into rich golden, orange, and red hues. Summer offers pleasant temperatures for higher altitude trails. In winter, the area is popular for snowshoeing, offering a different, magical experience.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the hiking trails in Bethmale?
A: While specific regulations can vary, generally, dogs are welcome on many hiking trails in the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with grazing animals or protected wildlife. It's always advisable to keep them on a leash and clean up after them. There are no explicit restrictions mentioned for the general hiking trails around Bethmale.

Q: Where can I park when hiking around Lac de Bethmale?
A: Parking is generally available near &lt;a href="https://www.tourisme-couserans-pyrenees.com/en/2025/12/17/lac-de-bethmale/" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, which serves as a common starting point for many trails. Specific parking areas are usually well-indicated for visitors accessing the lake and surrounding hiking routes.

Q: Are there any trails that lead to waterfalls in Bethmale?
A: Yes, the region offers trails that lead to waterfalls. For example, an easy 7 km loop to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/673275" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Étang d'Ayes&lt;/a&gt; via the Cirque de Campuls features a waterfall, providing a scenic destination for your hike.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Bethmale?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. Reviewers often praise the stunning emerald waters of Lake Bethmale, the diverse terrain ranging from lush forests to dramatic rocky landscapes, and the extensive views from mountain passes. The variety of trails catering to all abilities is also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes with significant elevation gain in Bethmale?
A: Yes, Bethmale offers several challenging hikes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5348086" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ayes Pond – Lake Bethmale loop from Étang de Bethmale&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 15.4 km trail with an elevation gain of about 770 meters. Another moderate option with substantial ascent is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956928524" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Etang d'Ayes – hike starting from Lac Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;, covering 12.4 km with 735 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I access hiking trails in Bethmale using public transport?
A: While Bethmale is nestled in a natural park, public transport options directly to trailheads can be limited. It is generally recommended to use a car to reach the main starting points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Bethmale&lt;/a&gt;. For detailed information on local transport, it's best to consult regional tourism resources.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for hiking in Bethmale?
A: No specific hiking permits are generally required for day hikes on the established trails within the Ariège Pyrenees Regional Natural Park around Bethmale. However, always respect local regulations, especially concerning protected areas or private land.

Q: Are there any cafes or accommodation options near the hiking trails in Bethmale?
A: While Bethmale is a more remote, natural area, there are typically small establishments or gîtes in the nearby villages that offer refreshments and accommodation. Around &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2483486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Bethmale&lt;/a&gt; itself, you might find picnic areas and facilities, but for cafes or hotels, you would usually need to venture into the closest hamlets or towns in the valley.
tips: Lake Bethmale is a pretty landscaped mountain lake, accessible by road and the GR 10.
Pretty pass on the GR 10 allowing access to the Ayes pond to the southwest and the Eychelle pond to the southeast.
This hike can also be done by "northern lights" like me. If you take your time and is reasonably sure-footed can experience here a tour with fantastic views!
